diagnosis: The balanced corpus and mean-pooling repair removed the v1 single-class and terminal-marker defects.
A linear head over the frozen Transformer representation still did not learn the custody decision rule above the preregistered floor.
Because the held-out denominator never opened, this is an instrument qualification failure rather than a claim attempt or an outcome-aware confirmatory retry.
The terminal instrument must expose the already recorded structured custody fields to the trainable state instead of asking one linear projection to recover them implicitly.

terminal_instrument_change.model_interface: L2-normalized frozen Qwen mean representation concatenated with an audited one-hot custody vector, consumed by a 32-unit nonlinear head
terminal_instrument_change.required_ablation: report Transformer-only, structured-only, and fused preflight accuracy

non_claims: No held-out v2 outcome was generated.
Structured custody features make the system decision surface explicit; they do not establish language-model unlearning.
No privacy, influence, erasure, transfer, support, or SOTA claim follows.
